title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Pembleton and Chief Inspector Kane discuss the well-planned nature of the murder. Kane identifies Monkswell Manor in Hapleydon, Berkshire, as a likely location of interest through a newspaper crossword puzzle, prompting Pembleton to contact the Berkshire Police Department.

At Monkswell Manor, which functions as a hotel, Major Metcalf arrives with two heavy leather trunks. A stiff, middle-aged man with a military background in India, Metcalf is a cousin of Mrs. Boyle. The innkeepers, Jeeves and Molly, are managing service when a blizzard begins. During the night, a bearded foreigner named Paravicini arrives after his car is overturned by snowdrifts; he possesses a powerful accent and a wallet full of money. Jeeves settles him into the green room. Giles warns that the heavy snow will soon leave the guests snowed in, cutting them off from the outside world and potentially disrupting telephone service and deliveries.

The following morning, five feet of snow has accumulated. Mrs. Boyle eats breakfast alone, feeling dissatisfied with the establishment. Seeking a social environment to replace the organized life she led during her wartime work, she finds the high quality of the food and service annoying because it prevents her from complaining. After breakfast, she encounters a red-haired young man with a mocking gaze, whom she deems mentally abnormal. Moving to the living room to claim a rose-colored chair, she expresses displeasure regarding a lukewarm radiator. As she watches snow slide off the roof, she loudly declares she will not stay long, prompting a soft chuckle from a young man named Reen.
